workflow.name: Generate and track offer letters with Google Sheets, Docs, Drive and Gmail
workflow.description:
## How it works
This workflow automates the full offer letter lifecycle, from generation to final candidate response tracking. When a new row with a Pending status is added to Google Sheets, it creates a personalized offer letter using a Google Docs template. The document is converted to PDF, stored in Google Drive, shared securely, and sent to the candidate via Gmail. Candidate responses are captured through webhooks, validated against deadlines, and used to update status and trigger follow-up communications.

### First set: Generate and send offer letter

- **Monitor new candidates**
  - **Google Sheets Trigger** – Watches for newly added rows in the offer sheet.
  - **If** – Ensures only rows with a Pending status are processed.

- **Generate offer letter**
  - **Google Drive** – Copies the offer letter template for the candidate.
  - **Google Docs** – Replaces placeholders with candidate-specific data.
  - **Google Drive** – Converts the document to a PDF file.

- **Store and share document**
  - **Google Drive** – Saves the PDF to a designated folder.
  - **Google Drive** – Assigns public read-only access to the file.

- **Send offer email**
  - **Gmail** – Sends the offer email with Accept and Decline action links.
  - **Google Sheets** – Updates the sheet with offer link, date, and Sent status.


### Second set: Capture response and finalize outcome

- **Capture candidate response**
  - **Webhook** – Receives the candidate’s decision from email buttons.
  - **Google Sheets** – Fetches the corresponding candidate record.
  - **If** – Checks whether the response is already locked.
  - **Code** – Validates decision against the response deadline.

- **Finalize outcome**
  - **Switch** – Routes Accepted, Rejected, or Timeout outcomes.
  - **Google Sheets** – Updates final status and locks further responses.
  - **Gmail** – Sends confirmation emails to the candidate and HR.

Dear {{ $('Google Sheets Trigger').item.json.Name }},

Congratulations! Please find attached your offer letter for the position of {{ $('Google Sheets Trigger').item.json.Role }} at Your Company, Inc.

Kindly review the document and share your confirmation or any questions you may have.

Offer Letter: Click here to view/download

Please respond by clicking one of the options below:

Accept Offer Decline Offer

Best regards,
ABC Person
HR, Your Company, Inc.

We appreciate the time you took to consider the offer at Your Company, Inc.

We understand that you have decided not to proceed with the offer. We appreciate the time and consideration you gave during the process.

We hope to stay in touch for future opportunities and wish you the very best in your career endeavors.

Thank you once again for your time and interest in Your Company, Inc.

Best regards,
ABC Person
HR, Your Company, Inc.

Please proceed with the necessary onboarding steps and update your records accordingly.

Best regards,
ABC Person
HR, Your Company, Inc.

We are delighted to know that you have accepted our offer at Your Company, Inc.

On behalf of the team, we would like to warmly welcome you aboard. We are excited to have you join us and look forward to working together.

. Please feel free to reach out if you have any questions before your start date.

Once again, thank you for accepting our offer. Welcome to the team!

Best regards,
ABC Person
HR, Your Company, Inc.

parameters.message:
=

Dear {{ $('Webhook1').item.json.query.name }},

Thank you for taking the time to review our offer.

As we did not receive a response within the allowed timeframe, the offer has now been closed and is no longer active. Any response submitted after the deadline cannot be considered.

We appreciate the time and interest you shared with us and wish you all the best in your future endeavors.

Kind regards,
Hiring Team
parameters.subject: Update regarding your application

parameters.content:
## Automated Offer Letter & Response Tracking Workflow

### How it works
This workflow automates the complete offer letter lifecycle.  
When a new candidate row is added to Google Sheets with a **Pending** status, the workflow generates a personalized offer letter using a Google Docs template. The document is filled with candidate details, converted to PDF, saved to Google Drive, shared with public read access, and emailed to the candidate.

The email contains **Accept** and **Decline** buttons. When the candidate clicks a button, a webhook captures the response. The workflow validates whether the response is still allowed (within the deadline and not already locked). Based on the response, the system updates the candidate status in Google Sheets and sends appropriate follow-up emails to both the candidate and HR.

If no response is received within the allowed time window, the offer is automatically marked as **Timeout**, locked, and a closure email is sent.

### Setup steps
1. Connect Google Sheets, Google Docs, Google Drive, and Gmail credentials  
2. Replace all placeholder IDs (Sheet ID, Drive Folder ID, Webhook paths)  
3. Ensure the Google Sheet has required columns (Id, Name, Email, Status, Date)  
4. Keep initial Status as **Pending** to trigger the workflow

### Customization tips
- Change response deadline by adjusting the date logic in the Code node  
- Edit email content to match your brand tone  
- Add Slack or WhatsApp notifications for HR if needed

## 1. Generate & Send Offer Letter

• Watches Google Sheets for new Pending entries  
• Creates offer letter from template  
• Converts to PDF, saves to Drive  
• Sends email with Accept / Decline buttons

## 2. Capture Response & Follow-Up

• Receives candidate response via webhook  
• Checks response validity & deadline  
• Updates final status in Google Sheets  
• Sends follow-up emails to candidate & HR

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
